# Darto — Full Documentation

> A minimal, fast web framework for Dart. This file concatenates the entire Darto documentation for ingestion by language models.

Source: https://darto-docs.vercel.app


---

# Getting Started

## Installation
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=installation -->

Add Darto to your pubspec.yaml:

```yaml
# pubspec.yaml
dependencies:
  darto: ^1.2.0
```

Or use the pub command directly:

```sh
dart pub add darto
```

## Quick Start
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=quick-start -->

Spin up a server in under a minute. Define a Darto instance, register a route, listen on a port.

```dart
import 'package:darto/darto.dart';

void main() {
  final app = Darto();

  app.get('/users/:id', [], (Context c) {
    final id = c.req.param('id');
    return c.ok({'id': id});
  });

  app.listen(3000, () => print('Listening on http://localhost:3000'));
}
```

> **Tip:** Use response helpers (c.ok, c.created, c.badRequest…) for clean status-code handling without manual headers.

## Core Concepts
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=core-concepts -->

Three typedefs are all you need to understand the entire framework:

```dart
typedef Handler    = FutureOr<Response>? Function(Context c);
typedef Middleware = FutureOr<void>      Function(Context c, Next next);
typedef Next       = Future<void>        Function();
```

- A handler receives a Context and returns a Response.
- A middleware receives a Context and a Next callback.
- Call await next() to continue the pipeline; return without it to short-circuit.

> **Success:** If you've used Hono, you already know Darto — the mental model is identical.


---

# API

## Application
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=application -->

### Creating the app

```dart
final app = Darto();             // default (non-strict trailing slash)
final app = Darto(strict: true); // /users ≠ /users/
```

### Global base path

```dart
final app = Darto().basePath('/v1');

app.get('/users', [], handler); // registered as /v1/users
```

### Starting and stopping

```dart
app.listen(3000);
app.listen(3000, () => print('ready'));

// Host binding, HTTPS/TLS and graceful-shutdown signals
await app.serve(port: 8080, host: 'localhost');
await app.listenSecure(443, securityContext);

await app.stop();                 // graceful drain of in-flight requests
int? port = app.port;             // bound port (e.g. after serve(port: 0))
bool running = app.isRunning;
```

By default serve / listen trap SIGINT/SIGTERM and shut down gracefully — pass shutdownSignals: false to opt out.

See: [HTTP verbs (app.get / post / …) → see Routing](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=routing)

## Routing
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=routing -->

### HTTP verbs

All verb methods take the middleware list as the second argument (required — pass [] for no middleware) and the handler last.

```dart
app.get(path, [], handler);
app.post(path, [], handler);
app.put(path, [], handler);
app.patch(path, [], handler);
app.delete(path, [], handler);

app.all(path, [], handler);
app.on(['GET', 'POST'], ['/a', '/b'], [], handler);
```

### Route parameters

```dart
app.get('/users/:id', [], (c) => c.ok({'id': c.req.param('id')}));
app.get('/posts/:slug?', [], handler);          // optional
app.get('/items/:id(\d+)', [], handler);        // regex constraint
app.get('/files/*path', [], handler);           // named wildcard
app.get('/assets/*', [], handler);              // unnamed wildcard
```

### Route groups

```dart
// Fluent chain
app.route('/users')
  .get([], listUsers)
  .post([auth()], createUser);

// Builder callback
app.route('/users', (r) {
  r.get('/', [], listUsers);
  r.post('/', [auth()], createUser);
  r.get('/:id', [], getUser);
});

// group() prefix
final api = app.group('/api');
api.get('/status', [], (c) => c.ok({'ok': true}));
```

> **Tip:** Groups compose: app.group('/api').group('/v2').get('/ping', [], …) registers GET /api/v2/ping.

> **Success:** Fast matcher: literal routes (no params or wildcards) match by a direct string compare on the dispatch hot path — only dynamic routes use the compiled matcher — and dispatch short-circuits on the HTTP method first. No configuration needed.

See: [Handler signature → see Context](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=context)

See: [Reading params / query → see Request](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=request)

## Context
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=context -->

The Context (c) is the single object every handler and middleware receives. It exposes per-request state, shortcuts to the incoming request and outgoing response, and metadata about the matched route.

### Reading the request

URL, headers, params, query and body — everything inbound lives on c.req.

See: [Full guide: Request (c.req)](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=request)

### Writing the response

Status codes, JSON / text / HTML / binary helpers, redirects, file sending and Response factories — everything outbound lives on c (and Response.*).

See: [Full guide: Response](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=response)

### Per-request state

| Member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| c.set('key', value) | Store per-request state for later middleware/handlers |
| c.get<T>('key') | Read state back, typed |
| c.user | Authenticated-user shortcut (Map<String, dynamic>?) — set by auth middleware |

```dart
c.set('userId', '42');
final id = c.get<String>('userId');

c.user = {'id': '42', 'role': 'admin'};
```

### Route metadata

| Member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| c.routePath | The matched route pattern, e.g. '/posts/:id' |
| c.basePath | The app's base path, e.g. '/api' |
| c.matchedRoutes | Every route that matched the request (middleware + handler) |

## Request (c.req)
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=request -->

Everything about the incoming request lives on c.req — URL, path parameters, query string, headers, body and per-request state.

### URL & method

| Member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| c.req.method | HTTP method (String) — GET, POST, … |
| c.req.path | Request path without the query string, e.g. /users/42 |
| c.req.url | The full request Uri (path, query and fragment) |
| c.req.ip | Remote client IP address (String) |

### Path parameters

Values captured from the route pattern, e.g. /users/:id.

| Member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| c.req.param('id') | Named path param as String? (null when absent) |
| c.req.paramInt('id') | Same, parsed as int? (null if missing/not a number) |
| c.req.paramDouble('id') | Parsed as double? |
| c.req.params() | All captured values as List<String?> |
| c.req.paramsMap | All params as an unmodifiable Map<String, String> |

### Query parameters

Read from the URL query string, e.g. ?page=2&active=true.

| Member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| c.req.query('q') | Query value as String? |
| c.req.queryInt('page') | Parsed as int? |
| c.req.queryDouble('rate') | Parsed as double? |
| c.req.queryBool('active') | true for 'true', '1', 'yes' or 'on' (case-insensitive) |
| c.req.queries() | All query values as List<String> |

### Headers

| Member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| c.req.header('authorization') | Header value as String? (name is case-insensitive) |
| c.req.headers | All headers as an unmodifiable Map<String, String> (multi-values joined by ', ') |

### Body

Read the request body once, in the shape you need.

| Member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| await c.req.json() | Parse a JSON body → Map<String, dynamic> |
| await c.req.json<User>(User.fromJson) | Parse a JSON body into a typed object |
| await c.req.text() | Body decoded as a UTF-8 String |
| await c.req.blob() | Body as raw bytes (Uint8List) |
| await c.req.arrayBuffer() | Body as a ByteBuffer |
| await c.req.parseBody({saveDir}) | multipart / urlencoded → Map; files come back as UploadedFile (pass saveDir to stream them to disk) |
| await c.req.formData() | Form body — Map for urlencoded, String for multipart |
| c.req.body | The raw body stream (Stream<List<int>>), consumed once |

```dart
final data   = await c.req.json();
final user   = await c.req.json<User>(User.fromJson);
final upload = await c.req.parseBody(saveDir: 'uploads');
```

> **Warning:** Read the body once: json(), text(), blob() and arrayBuffer() share a single cached read, but the raw body stream and multipart parseBody() consume the stream directly — don't mix the two on the same request.

### Validated data & state

| Member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| c.req.valid<T>('json') | Data stored by validator() / zValidator() for a target ('json', 'query', 'param', 'form', 'header') |
| c.req.get('key') | Read per-request state set earlier in the pipeline |
| c.req.set('key', value) | Store per-request state for later middleware/handlers |

See: [Multipart bodies → see File Upload](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=file-upload)

## Response
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=response -->

Two layers for sending a response. c.* helpers cover the common cases ergonomically; Response.* factories give you full control over status, content-type and headers when you need it.

### Helpers on c.*

| Helper | Description |
| --- | --- |
| c.ok([body]) | 200 OK |
| c.created([body]) | 201 Created |
| c.noContent() | 204 No Content |
| c.badRequest([body]) | 400 Bad Request |
| c.unauthorized([body]) | 401 Unauthorized |
| c.forbidden([body]) | 403 Forbidden |
| c.notFound([body]) | 404 Not Found |
| c.conflict([body]) | 409 Conflict |
| c.internalError([body]) | 500 Internal Server Error |
| c.json(data, [status]) | JSON response (application/json) |
| c.text(str, [status]) | Plain text (text/plain) |
| c.html(str, [status]) | HTML (text/html) |
| c.status(code) | Set the status, then chain .json() / .text() / .html() |
| c.redirect(url, [status]) | Redirect (302 by default) |
| c.binary(bytes, {status, contentType}) | Binary response |
| c.body(data, [status], [headers]) | HonoJS-style raw body — text / bytes / null |

```dart
app.get('/users/:id', [], (c) {
  final user = users[c.req.param('id')];
  return user == null ? c.notFound({'error': 'not found'}) : c.ok(user);
});
```

### Response headers

| Member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| c.header(name, value) | Set a response header |
| c.statusCode | Current response status code (int) |

### Response factories

Build a Response directly when you need full control over status, content-type and headers — the c.* helpers above call these internally.

| Factory | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Response.json(data, {status, headers}) | JSON response (application/json); encodes Maps/Lists and converts DateTime to ISO-8601 |
| Response.text(str, {status, headers}) | Plain text (text/plain; charset=utf-8) |
| Response.html(str, {status, headers}) | HTML (text/html; charset=utf-8) |
| Response.bytes(bytes, {status, contentType, headers}) | Binary body with a custom content-type (default application/octet-stream) |
| Response.empty({status}) | No body (defaults to 204 No Content) |

```dart
app.get('/raw', [], (c) => Response.json({'ok': true}, status: 201));
```

### Sending files

| Helper | Description |
| --- | --- |
| await c.file(path, {contentType}) | Stream a file — sets Content-Length automatically |
| await c.download(path, {filename}) | Force download via Content-Disposition |

See: [Full guide: File Download](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=file-download)


---

# Helpers

## Helpers
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=helpers -->

### Cookie

Cookie helpers are standalone functions imported from package:darto/cookie.dart.

```dart
import 'package:darto/cookie.dart';

setCookie(c, 'session', token,
  CookieOptions(maxAge: 3600, httpOnly: true, secure: true));
final sid = getCookie(c, 'session');
deleteCookie(c, 'session');

// Signed cookies (HMAC-SHA256)
await setSignedCookie(c, 'session', token, secret);
final val = await getSignedCookie(c, secret, 'session');
```

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| setCookie(c, name, value, [opts]) | Add a Set-Cookie header (multiple cookies stack) |
| getCookie(c, name) | Read a cookie sent by the client (String?) |
| getCookies(c) | All client cookies as Map<String, String> |
| deleteCookie(c, name) | Expire a cookie |
| setSignedCookie(c, name, value, secret, [opts]) | Set an HMAC-SHA256 signed cookie |
| getSignedCookie(c, secret, name) | Read & verify a signed cookie — null if tampered |
| CookieOptions(path, domain, maxAge, expires, httpOnly, secure, sameSite) | Cookie attributes |

### JWT helpers

sign() and verify() are async. Include exp in the payload to set an expiry.

```dart
import 'package:darto/jwt.dart';

final token = await sign(
  {'sub': '42', 'exp': DateTime.now().add(Duration(hours: 1)).millisecondsSinceEpoch ~/ 1000},
  env.jwtSecret,
);
final payload = await verify(token, env.jwtSecret);
```

### Proxy

proxy() is a route handler helper — call it inside a handler and return its result. A single wildcard route covers exact and deep paths.

```dart
import 'package:darto/proxy.dart';

// /* matches both the exact path and any sub-path:
// /api/users  •  /api/users/1  •  /api/users/1/posts
app.all('/api/users/*', [], (Context c) =>
    proxy(c, 'https://backend.com${c.req.path}'));

// With header overrides
app.all('/v1/*', [], (Context c) =>
    proxy(c, 'https://example.com${c.req.path}',
        options: ProxyOptions(
          headers: {
            'X-Proxy-By': 'darto-gateway',
            'Authorization': 'Bearer INTERNAL_SECRET',
            'Cookie': null, // null = remove header
          },
        )));
```

### Health checks

health() builds a liveness/readiness Handler. Returns 200 {status: 'ok'} when every check passes, or 503 {status: 'unavailable', checks: {...}} when any fails. A throwing check counts as down.

```dart
import 'package:darto/health.dart';

// Liveness — 200 while the process is up
app.get('/healthz', [], health());

// Readiness — 503 until dependencies are reachable
app.get('/readyz', [], health(
  checks: {'db': () => db.ping(), 'cache': () => redis.ping()},
  info: () => {'version': '1.0.0'},
));
```

### Streaming (binary, text, SSE)

Return one of stream() / streamText() / streamSSE() from a handler to push response chunks over a kept-alive connection. The writer exposes write / pipe / sleep, and onAbort fires when the client disconnects.

```dart
import 'package:darto/stream.dart';

// Server-Sent Events — push live updates to the browser
app.get('/sse', [], (c) => streamSSE(c, (w) async {
  w.onAbort(() => print('client gone'));
  for (var i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
    await w.writeSSE(SseEvent(event: 'tick', data: '$i'));
    await w.sleep(const Duration(seconds: 1));
  }
}));

// Text stream — line-by-line
app.get('/log', [], (c) => streamText(c, (w) async {
  await w.writeln('starting…');
  await w.pipe(linesOf('build.log'));
}));
```

| Helper | Description |
| --- | --- |
| stream(c, cb, {onError}) | Binary stream — writer is DartoStreamWriter (write / pipe) |
| streamText(c, cb, {onError}) | Text stream — writer is DartoTextStreamWriter (write / writeln / pipe) |
| streamSSE(c, cb, {onError}) | Server-Sent Events — writer is DartoSSEWriter (writeSSE / sleep) |
| SseEvent({data, event, id, retry}) | Payload for one SSE message |
| writer.onAbort(cb) | Fires when the client closes the connection |

### Route introspection

Inspect the routing decision the framework made for the current request — useful for logging, metrics or debugging.

```dart
import 'package:darto/route.dart';

app.get('/posts/:id', [], (c) {
  print(routePath(c));      // '/posts/:id'
  print(basePath(c));       // '/' (or the app's basePath)
  print(matchedRoutes(c));  // every route that fired, mw + handler
  return c.ok({});
});
```

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| matchedRoutes(c) | List<RouteSpec> of every route that matched (middleware + handler) |
| routePath(c) | Pattern of the matched handler — e.g. '/posts/:id' |
| baseRoutePath(c) | Group prefix pattern of the matched route |
| basePath(c) | The app's base path |

### Dev — routes printer

showRoutes(app) pretty-prints every registered route with colored HTTP verbs — handy during development to verify wiring at a glance.

```dart
import 'package:darto/dev.dart';

final app = Darto();
app.get('/users', [], handler);
app.post('/users', [], handler);

showRoutes(app); // GET  /users
                 // POST /users
app.listen(3000);
```


---

# Middlewares

## Middleware
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=middleware -->

### Registering

use(middleware) registers a global middleware that runs on every request. mount(path, middleware) registers a path-scoped middleware — it only runs when the request path starts with path (supports * wildcards).

```dart
app.use(logger());               // global — runs on every request
app.use(cors());                 // call separately per middleware
app.mount('/api/*', jwt(...));   // path-scoped
app.get('/admin', [requireAdmin()], handler); // route-level
```

### Writing one

```dart
Middleware timer() => (Context c, Next next) async {
  final sw = Stopwatch()..start();
  await next();
  print('${c.req.method} ${c.req.path}  ${sw.elapsedMilliseconds}ms');
};
```

### Short-circuit

```dart
Middleware requireAdmin() => (c, next) async {
  if (c.user?['role'] != 'admin') {
    c.forbidden({'error': 'Admins only'});
    return; // pipeline stops here
  }
  await next();
};
```

## Built-in Middlewares
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=middleware-builtin -->

Darto ships with batteries: logger, CORS, JWT, Basic & Bearer auth, cache, compress, CSRF, body-limit, rate-limit, request ID, ETag, RBAC and more.

```dart
import 'package:darto/logger.dart';
import 'package:darto/cors.dart';
import 'package:darto/jwt.dart';
import 'package:darto/basic_auth.dart';
import 'package:darto/bearer_auth.dart';
import 'package:darto/cache.dart';

app.use(logger());
app.mount('/api/*', cors(origin: 'https://example.com'));
app.mount('/api/*', jwt(secret: env.jwtSecret));
app.mount('/admin/*', basicAuth(username: 'admin', password: 'secret'));
app.mount('/api/*', bearerAuth(token: ['key1', 'key2']));
```

### RBAC

```dart
import 'package:darto/require_roles.dart';
app.delete('/users/:id', [requireRoles(['admin'])], handler);
```

### Body limit

```dart
app.post('/upload', [bodyLimit(maxSize: 5 * 1024 * 1024)], handler);
```

### Rate limit

```dart
import 'package:darto/rate_limit.dart';

// 100 requests / minute per client IP (in-memory, zero-dep)
app.use(rateLimit(max: 100, window: Duration(minutes: 1)));

// Per-user, custom rejection — pass a RateLimitStore for a distributed backend
app.mount('/api/*', rateLimit(
  max: 20,
  keyGenerator: (c) => c.user?['id'] ?? c.req.ip,
  onLimitExceeded: (c) => c.status(429).json({'error': 'slow down'}),
));
```

### Request ID

```dart
import 'package:darto/request_id.dart';

app.use(requestId()); // honors an incoming X-Request-Id, else a UUID v4
app.get('/', [], (c) => c.ok({'id': requestIdOf(c)}));
```

### ETag

```dart
import 'package:darto/etag.dart';

app.use(etag()); // hashes dynamic responses → ETag + 304 on If-None-Match
```

### Health check

health() is a handler factory (package:darto/health.dart). Returns 200 when every named check passes, or 503 with the failing checks.

```dart
import 'package:darto/health.dart';

app.get('/healthz', [], health());
app.get('/readyz', [], health(
  checks: {'db': () => db.ping(), 'cache': () => redis.ping()},
  info: () => {'version': '1.2.0'},
));
```

> **Warning:** When using jwt, always set verifyOptions (iss, exp, nbf) for production deployments.

## Session
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=session -->

Cookie-based signed sessions. Data is JSON-serialised, base64url-encoded, and signed with HMAC-SHA256 — tamper-proof but not encrypted. Store only non-sensitive identifiers (e.g. userId) in the session.

```dart
import 'package:darto/session.dart';

// Register once globally — reads and validates the session cookie on every request
app.use(sessionMiddleware(
  secret: 'at-least-32-chars-long-secret!!',
  duration: 60 * 30,           // cookie maxAge in seconds (default: 1800)
  cookieName: 'darto.session', // optional, this is the default
));
```

### Write / Read / Delete

```dart
app.post('/login', [], (c) async {
  final body = await c.req.json();
  await sessionContext(c).update({'userId': body['id'], 'role': 'user'});
  return c.ok({'message': 'logged in'});
});

app.get('/me', [], (c) {
  final data = sessionContext(c).get(); // null if no active session
  if (data == null) return c.unauthorized({'error': 'no session'});
  return c.ok(data);
});

app.post('/logout', [], (c) {
  sessionContext(c).delete();
  return c.ok({'message': 'logged out'});
});
```

| Method | Returns | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| sessionContext(c).get() | Map<String, dynamic>? | Session data — null if no valid session |
| sessionContext(c).update(data) | Future<void> | Replace data and write the signed cookie |
| sessionContext(c).delete() | void | Clear data and remove the cookie |

> **Warning:** Session data is visible (base64-decoded) but not alterable — the HMAC signature prevents tampering. Do not store passwords or secrets inside the session.

## Validator (core)
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=validator -->

validator is the core validation middleware (from package:darto/validator.dart) — no extra package needed. You pass a callback that receives the raw value and the Context: return a Response to short-circuit with any status code, or return data to store it for c.req.valid().

### Bring your own logic

To validate with zard schemas, just add the zard package — you do NOT need darto_validator (that one is only for zValidator). See the zValidator section under Plugins for the schema-driven alternative.

```dart
import 'package:darto/validator.dart';
import 'package:zard/zard.dart'; // add zard to pubspec — for z.*

final loginSchema = z.map({
  'email':    z.string().email(),
  'password': z.string().min(6),
});

// 401 on failure — you decide the status code
app.post('/login', [
  validator('json', (value, c) {
    final result = loginSchema.safeParse(value);
    if (!result.success) return c.status(401).json({'errors': result.error?.format()});
    return result.data;
  }),
], (c) {
  final credentials = c.req.valid<Map<String, dynamic>>('json');
  return c.ok({'message': 'Welcome, ${credentials[\'email\']}!'});
});
```

### Targets

- 'json' — request body
- 'query' — query string
- 'param' — path parameters
- 'form' — url-encoded / multipart
- 'header' — request headers

> **Warning:** Always validate untrusted input at the edges — bodies, queries, headers and uploaded files.

See: [Schema-driven alternative: zValidator (darto_validator)](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-validator)


---

# Database

## Database & ORM
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=database -->

Darto is unopinionated about persistence — it's a web framework, so you can use any database package you like (raw sqlite3, a driver, or any ORM). For a type-safe, batteries-included experience the officially recommended ORM is Dartonic.

### Dartonic (recommended ORM)

Dartonic is a type-safe SQL query builder and ORM for Dart, inspired by Drizzle. You define your schema once as Dart classes and query SQLite, PostgreSQL or MySQL through a fully-typed, fluent API — no code generation, no dynamic.

> **Tip:** One schema, three databases. Pair dartonic_core with a driver (dartonic_sqlite / dartonic_postgres / dartonic_mysql) — you only ship what you use.

- [Dartonic documentation](https://dartonic.vercel.app/)
- [pub.dev/packages/dartonic_core](https://pub.dev/packages/dartonic_core)

### Validation with Zard

For request validation the recommended library is Zard — a Zod-inspired schema validator. Wire it into Darto with darto_validator, which gives you the zValidator middleware (400 on invalid input) and errorBridge (maps thrown/typed errors — including Dartonic's DatabaseError — to HTTP status codes).

- [Zard documentation](https://zard-docs.vercel.app/)
- [pub.dev/packages/zard](https://pub.dev/packages/zard)
- [pub.dev/packages/darto_validator](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_validator)

### End-to-end example

A Dartonic table as the source of truth, Zard validating the request, and errorBridge turning a duplicate email into a 409:

```dart
// main.dart
import 'package:darto/darto.dart';
import 'package:darto_validator/darto_validator.dart'; // zValidator + errorBridge
import 'package:dartonic_core/dartonic_core.dart';
import 'package:dartonic_sqlite/dartonic_sqlite.dart';

class Users extends Table {
  final id    = integer('id').primaryKey(autoIncrement: true);
  final email = text('email').notNull().unique();
  final name  = text('name').notNull();
}
final users = Users();

void main() async {
  final db = await connectSqlite('app.db', schemas: [users]);
  final app = Darto();
  app.use(errorBridge()); // DB errors (e.g. unique) -> HTTP (409/...)

  final schema = z.map({
    'email': z.string().email(),
    'name': z.string().min(1),
  });

  app.post('/users', [zValidator('json', schema)], (Context c) async {
    final data = c.req.valid<Map<String, dynamic>>('json');
    final rows = await db.insert(users).valuesRaw(data).returning();
    return c.created(rows.first.raw);
  });

  app.listen(3000);
}
```


---

# Official Plugins

## CLI Tools
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=cli-tools -->

See: [Package: darto_cli](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-cli)

Install the Darto CLI globally with pub:

```sh
dart pub global activate darto_cli
```

Make sure ~/.pub-cache/bin is on your PATH.

### Create a project

```sh
darto create my_api          # with starter user module
darto create my_api --blank  # minimal — no modules, just GET /health
```

Generated structure (default):

```sh
my_api/
  bin/server.dart
  lib/
    app.dart
    modules/user/
      user_controller.dart   # handlers + route registration
      user_service.dart      # business logic
  pubspec.yaml
```

### Dev server

Watches lib/, bin/, and src/ recursively. Restarts automatically (350 ms debounce) on any .dart file change — including deep subdirectories.

```sh
darto dev
darto dev bin/server.dart
```

### Build & Start

```sh
darto build                       # compile to build/server + Dockerfile
darto build --output build/api    # custom output path
darto build --no-docker           # skip Dockerfile
darto start                       # run the compiled binary
```

### Generate typed Flutter client

```sh
darto gen client flutter
darto gen client flutter --base-url https://api.example.com --output lib/api_client.dart
```

> **Tip:** darto gen reads createApp() from lib/app.dart, introspects all registered routes, and emits a typed ApiClient with one sub-module class per route group.

## darto_cli
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-cli -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_cli)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_cli)

Official CLI — scaffold projects, run a hot-reload dev server, build native executables and generate a typed API client.

### Install

```sh
dart pub global activate darto_cli
```

See: [Full guide: CLI Tools](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=cli-tools)

## darto_validator
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-validator -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_validator)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_validator)

Request validation powered by zard (Zod-style) via the zValidator middleware. Pairs schema-driven validation with automatic 400 responses and a c.req.valid<T>() typed read. Also converts schemas to OpenAPI with schema.toOpenApiSchema().

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_validator: ^1.1.0
```

### zValidator — schema-driven

```dart
import 'package:darto_validator/darto_validator.dart';

final body = z.map({
  'email': z.string().email(),
  'age':   z.int().min(18),
});

app.post('/users', [zValidator('json', body)], (c) {
  final data = c.req.valid<Map<String, dynamic>>('json');
  return c.created(data);
});
```

### Custom error hook

```dart
zValidator('json', body, (result, c) {
  if (!result.success) {
    return c.status(422).json({
      'error': 'Validation failed',
      'issues': result.error?.format(),
    });
  }
  return null;
});
```

> **Tip:** If you only need the core validator (no zard schema), you don't need this package — see Middlewares → Validator (core).

See: [Core alternative: validator (no plugin needed)](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=validator)

## darto_openapi
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-openapi -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_openapi)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_openapi)

OpenAPI 3.1 spec generation + Scalar API docs. Describe a route once: it validates the request and is documented from the same source.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_openapi: ^1.0.0
```

### Usage

```dart
import 'package:darto_openapi/darto_openapi.dart';

final api = OpenApi(app, info: Info(title: 'API', version: '1.0.0'));

api.post('/posts',
  request: Req(json: Schema.object({'title': Schema.string(minLength: 1)}, required: ['title'])),
  responses: {201: Res('Created')},
  handler: (c) => c.created(c.req.valid('json')),
);

app.use(api.docs()); // serves /openapi.json + /docs (Scalar)
```

### Schema builders

| Builder | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Schema.string({minLength, maxLength, format}) | String — format: 'email' | 'uri' | 'date-time' | … |
| Schema.integer({minimum, maximum}) | Integer number |
| Schema.number({minimum}) | Floating-point number |
| Schema.boolean() | Boolean |
| Schema.array(schema, {minItems}) | Array of items matching schema |
| Schema.object({fields}, {required}) | Object with named properties |
| Schema.raw(Map) | Pass raw OpenAPI schema through unchanged |

### Security schemes

```dart
final api = OpenApi(app,
  info: Info(title: 'My API', version: '1.0.0'),
  securitySchemes: {
    'bearer': SecurityScheme.bearer(),
    'apiKey': SecurityScheme.apiKey(name: 'x-api-key', location: 'header'),
  },
);

api.get('/me',
  security: ['bearer'],
  responses: {200: Res('User profile')},
  handler: (c) => c.ok({'user': 'me'}),
);
```

### Typed client

generateDartClient(spec) turns the OpenAPI document into an end-to-end typed Dart client — model classes plus a typed method per operation (dependency-free).

```dart
final src = generateDartClient(api.toJson(), baseUrl: 'https://api.example.com');
File('lib/api_client.dart').writeAsStringSync(src);

// in the generated client:
final post = await ApiClient().postPosts(PostPostsRequest(title: 'Hello'));
print(post.id); // typed
```

## darto_zard_openapi
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-zard-openapi -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_zard_openapi)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_zard_openapi)

Hono-style zod-openapi for Darto. Define a zard schema once: it validates the request (real zard — email, refine, coerce, custom messages) and generates the OpenAPI 3.1 document from the same source. A composable plugin: OpenAPIDarto wraps your own Darto app.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto: ^1.2.0
  darto_zard_openapi: ^1.0.0
```

### Usage

```dart
import 'package:darto/darto.dart';
import 'package:darto_zard_openapi/darto_zard_openapi.dart';

final app = Darto();            // your own Darto app
final api = OpenAPIDarto(app);  // plug OpenAPI on top

// A reusable route contract, decoupled from the handler.
final getUser = createRoute(
  method: 'get',
  path: '/users/:id',
  request: Req(params: z.map({'id': z.coerce.int().min(1)}).openapiSchema()),
  responses: [Res(200, 'Found', body: userSchema), Res(404, 'Not found')],
);

api.openapi(getUser, [], (c) {
  final id = c.req.valid<Map<String, dynamic>>('param')['id'];
  return c.ok({'id': id});
});

api.doc('/openapi.json', info: Info(title: 'API', version: '1.0.0'));
app.get('/docs', [], scalarUI(url: '/openapi.json')); // Scalar UI
```

### Schema metadata

Add per-field metadata with .openapi(example:, description:) — example is type-checked against the field's type and stays correct through coerce/transform. Name a reusable component with .openapiSchema('Name'), emitted under #/components/schemas and referenced with $ref.

```dart
final userSchema = z.map({
  'name': z.string().min(2).openapi(example: 'Ada', description: 'Full name'),
  'age':  z.int().min(0).max(150).openapi(example: 28),
  'role': z.$enum(['admin', 'user']).openapi(description: 'Role'),
}).openapiSchema('User'); // -> #/components/schemas/User
```

> **Tip:** Validation is done by the zard schemas (via Darto's validator middleware) — full zard power, with the same schema feeding the spec. Requires zard ^1.3.0 and darto_validator ^1.3.0.

See: [Lower-level alternative: darto_openapi (native Schema builders)](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-openapi)

## darto_test
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-test -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_test)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_test)

Ergonomic test client — boot an app on an ephemeral port and assert responses without managing a server (supertest-style).

### Install

```yaml
dev_dependencies:
  darto_test: ^1.0.0
```

### Usage

```dart
import 'package:darto_test/darto_test.dart';

final client = await TestClient.create(buildApp());

final res = await client.get('/hello');
expect(res.statusCode, 200);
expect(res.json['msg'], 'hi');

await client.close();
```

### setUp / tearDown pattern

```dart
import 'package:darto_test/darto_test.dart';
import 'package:test/test.dart';

void main() {
  late TestClient client;

  setUp(() async => client = await TestClient.create(buildApp()));
  tearDown(() => client.close());

  test('GET /hello returns 200', () async {
    final res = await client.get('/hello');
    expect(res.statusCode, 200);
    expect(res.json['msg'], 'hi');
  });

  test('POST /users creates a user', () async {
    final res = await client.post('/users', json: {'name': 'Alice'});
    expect(res.statusCode, 201);
    expect(res.json['name'], 'Alice');
  });
}
```

### json: vs body:

json: accepts a Map/List, encodes it with jsonEncode and sets Content-Type: application/json automatically. body: sends the value raw — a String is written as-is, a List<int> is sent as bytes. Use json: for APIs (the common case), body: when you need to control the payload format.

```dart
// json: → auto-encode + Content-Type: application/json
client.post('/users', json: {'name': 'Alice'});

// body: → raw String (no Content-Type set)
client.post('/login', body: 'username=alice&password=s3cret',
  headers: {'content-type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'});

// body: → raw bytes
client.post('/upload', body: imageBytes);
```

### TestClient API

| Member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| TestClient.create(app) | Boots the app on a free loopback port; returns a client |
| client.get / head / options(path, {headers}) | Request without a body |
| client.post / put / patch / delete(path, {headers, body, json}) | Request with an optional body |
| client.request(method, path, {...}) | Generic request |
| client.port | The bound ephemeral port |
| client.close() | Stops the app and closes the client |

### TestResponse API

| Member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| res.statusCode | HTTP status code |
| res.body | Raw response body as UTF-8 text |
| res.json | Body parsed as JSON (null when empty) |
| res.header(name) | Response header value (case-insensitive) |
| res.headers | All headers as Map<String, String> |
| res.cookie(name) / res.cookies | Set-Cookie lookup / full list |
| res.isOk | true for 2xx status codes |

## darto_logger
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-logger -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_logger)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_logger)

Structured logging — JSON or pretty output, levels, bound fields, and a request-logging middleware with request-id correlation.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_logger: ^1.0.0
```

### Usage

```dart
import 'package:darto/request_id.dart';
import 'package:darto_logger/darto_logger.dart';

final log = Logger();

app.use(requestId());        // adds X-Request-Id
app.use(requestLogger(log)); // logs each request with that id

app.get('/', [], (c) {
  log.info('home hit', {'q': c.req.query('q')});
  return c.ok({'ok': true});
});
```

### Child loggers

child() returns a new Logger with fields bound to every subsequent message — useful for adding context (request-id, user-id, service name) once instead of repeating it.

```dart
final reqLog = log.child({'requestId': c.req.header('x-request-id')});
reqLog.info('processing order', {'orderId': 42});
// → {level: info, msg: "processing order", requestId: "…", orderId: 42}
```

### API

| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Logger({minLevel, pretty, output}) | Creates a logger; pretty=true for human-readable output |
| log.debug / info / warn / error(msg, [fields]) | Log at the given level with optional fields |
| log.child(fields) | Returns a new Logger with bound fields added to every message |
| LogLevel.debug / info / warn / error | Enum — messages below minLevel are dropped |
| requestLogger(logger) | Middleware — logs method, path, status, duration per request |

## darto_auth
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-auth -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_auth)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_auth)

Authentication — password hashing (PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256, no native deps) and session-based auth guards built on Darto's session middleware.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_auth: ^1.0.0
```

### Password hashing

```dart
import 'package:darto_auth/darto_auth.dart';

final hash = hashPassword('s3cret');         // store this
final ok   = verifyPassword('s3cret', hash); // true (constant-time)
```

### Session auth

```dart
import 'package:darto/session.dart';
import 'package:darto_auth/darto_auth.dart';

app.use(sessionMiddleware(secret: env.sessionSecret));

app.post('/login', [], (c) async {
  final body = await c.req.json();
  final user = await users.findByEmail(body['email']);
  if (user == null || !verifyPassword(body['password'], user.hash)) {
    return c.unauthorized({'error': 'invalid credentials'});
  }
  await signIn(c, {'id': user.id});
  return c.ok({'ok': true});
});

app.get('/me', [authGuard()], (c) => c.ok(authUser(c)));
```

| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
| hashPassword / verifyPassword | PBKDF2 hash & constant-time verify |
| PasswordHasher({iterations, saltLength}) | Configurable hasher |
| signIn(c, user) / signOut(c) | Authenticate / clear the session |
| authUser(c) | The session user, or null |
| authGuard({onUnauthorized}) | Middleware — 401 when unauthenticated; sets c.user otherwise |

### OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect

OAuthProvider runs the Authorization-Code flow with PKCE S256 + randomised state (CSRF). Factories for Google (OIDC) and GitHub. provider.attach(app, prefix, onSignIn: ...) registers both /start and /callback routes in one call.

```dart
import 'package:darto/session.dart';
import 'package:darto_auth/darto_auth.dart';

final google = await OAuthProvider.google(
  clientId: env.googleClientId,
  clientSecret: env.googleClientSecret,
  redirectUri: 'http://localhost:3000/auth/google/callback',
);

app.use(sessionMiddleware(secret: env.sessionSecret));

google.attach(app, '/auth/google', onSignIn: (c, user) async {
  await signIn(c, {'id': user.id, 'email': user.email, 'name': user.name});
  return c.redirect('/');
});
```

| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
| OAuthProvider(...) | Generic OAuth2 provider |
| OAuthProvider.oidc({issuer, ...}) | OIDC discovery from /.well-known/openid-configuration |
| OAuthProvider.google({...}) / .github({...}) | Pre-configured factories |
| provider.attach(app, prefix, {onSignIn, failureRedirect}) | Register /start + /callback in one call |
| OAuthUser | Normalised (id, email, name, picture, raw) profile |
| pkceVerifier() / pkceChallenge(v) | PKCE helpers (S256) |

> **Tip:** id_token claims are decoded but not verified against the issuer's JWKS — authenticity comes from TLS to the token endpoint. JWKS verification is on the roadmap as opt-in.

## darto_inject
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-di -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_inject)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_inject)

Typed dependency injection — Provider<T> factories with app- and request-scope, lifecycle hooks (onDispose), test overrides, and a built-in contextProvider. No build_runner, no decorators.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_inject: ^1.0.0
```

### Declare providers

```dart
import 'package:darto_inject/darto_inject.dart';

final envProvider = Provider<Env>((di) => Env.fromFile('.env'));

final dbProvider = Provider<Db>(
  (di) => Db.connect(di.read(envProvider).dbUrl),
  onDispose: (db) => db.close(),
);

final userServiceProvider = Provider<UserService>(
  (di) => UserService(di.read(dbProvider)),
);
```

### Install on the app

```dart
final di = Di(providers: [envProvider, dbProvider, userServiceProvider]);
await di.warmup(); // eagerly build app-scope singletons

final app = Darto()..use(di.middleware());

app.get('/users', [], (c) {
  final svc = c.read(userServiceProvider);
  return c.ok(svc.list());
});

app.listen(3000);
await di.dispose(); // runs every onDispose in reverse order
```

### Request scope + contextProvider

Mark a provider with scope: Scope.request and read contextProvider inside it to derive per-request values without leaking the Context into your services.

```dart
final currentUserProvider = AsyncProvider<User?>(
  (di) async {
    final c = di.read(contextProvider);
    final token = c.req.header('authorization')?.replaceFirst('Bearer ', '');
    return token == null ? null : await di.read(userServiceProvider).fromToken(token);
  },
  scope: Scope.request,
);

app.get('/me', [], (c) async => c.ok(await c.readAsync(currentUserProvider)));
```

### Test overrides

```dart
final di = Di(providers: [userServiceProvider])
  ..override(userServiceProvider, (di) => FakeUserService());
```

### Feature

```dart
final userFeature = Feature(
  providers: [userServiceProvider],
  routes: (r) {
    r.get('/users', [], listUsers);
    r.post('/users', [authGuard()], createUser);
  },
);

app.install('/api', userFeature);
```

### CLI scaffolds

```sh
darto gen feature users    # lib/features/users/users_feature.dart
darto gen service mailer   # lib/services/mailer_service.dart
```

| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Provider<T>(factory, {scope, onDispose}) | Synchronous typed factory |
| AsyncProvider<T>(factory, {scope, onDispose}) | Asynchronous typed factory |
| Scope.app / Scope.request | Lifetime of the cached instance |
| Di({providers, asyncProviders}) | Container — caches, overrides, middleware |
| Di.warmup() / dispose() | Eager build / reverse-order cleanup |
| Di.override(p, factory) | Replace a factory (tests) |
| c.read(p) / c.readAsync(p) | Resolve a provider on the current request |
| contextProvider | Built-in Provider<Context> for request-scope factories |
| Feature({providers, routes}) | Providers + routes bundle |
| app.install([prefix], feature) | Mount a feature, optionally prefixed |

## darto_cache
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-cache -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_cache)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_cache)

Cache primitives — a tiny Cache interface with a zero-dep MemoryCache (LRU + TTL) and a RedisCache adapter for shared / distributed caching.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_cache: ^1.0.0
```

### In-process: MemoryCache

```dart
import 'package:darto_cache/darto_cache.dart';

final cache = MemoryCache(maxEntries: 1024);

await cache.set('user:42', {'name': 'Eva'}, ttl: Duration(minutes: 5));
final user = await cache.get<Map<String, dynamic>>('user:42');
```

### Distributed: RedisCache

```dart
final cache = await RedisCache.connect(
  host: 'localhost',
  port: 6379,
  prefix: 'app:',
);

await cache.set('user:42', {'name': 'Eva'}, ttl: Duration(minutes: 5));
final user = await cache.get<Map<String, dynamic>>('user:42');
await cache.close();
```

### Read-through with remember

The call site you want 90% of the time: builder runs on miss, the value is stored with ttl, then returned. null is not cached.

```dart
final user = await cache.remember<Map<String, dynamic>>(
  'user:$id',
  ttl: Duration(minutes: 5),
  builder: () => db.users.findById(id),
);
```

### Wiring with darto_inject

```dart
final cacheProvider = AsyncProvider<Cache>(
  (di) => RedisCache.connect(
    host: di.read(envProvider).redisHost,
    prefix: 'app:',
  ),
  onDispose: (c) => c.close(),
);

app.get('/users/:id', [], (c) async {
  final cache = await c.readAsync(cacheProvider);
  final user = await cache.remember(
    'user:${c.req.param('id')}',
    ttl: Duration(minutes: 5),
    builder: () => userService.findById(c.req.param('id')!),
  );
  return c.ok(user);
});
```

| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Cache (interface) | get / set / delete / has / clear / close |
| cache.remember(key, {ttl, builder}) | Read-through helper |
| MemoryCache({maxEntries}) | In-process; LRU when maxEntries is set |
| RedisCache.connect({host, port, prefix}) | Distributed cache over Redis |
| prefix (Redis) | Namespaces the keys; clear() only drops keys under prefix |

## darto_rate_limit
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-rate-limit -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_rate_limit)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_rate_limit)

Distributed RateLimitStore for the core rateLimit() middleware. The in-process store is fine for one instance; this package adds a Redis-backed store so multiple replicas behind a load balancer share the same counter.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto: ^1.2.0
  darto_rate_limit: ^1.0.0
```

### Usage

```dart
import 'package:darto/darto.dart';
import 'package:darto/rate_limit.dart';
import 'package:darto_rate_limit/darto_rate_limit.dart';

final store = await RedisRateLimitStore.connect(
  host: 'localhost',
  port: 6379,
  prefix: 'rl:',
);

app.use(rateLimit(
  max: 100,
  window: Duration(minutes: 1),
  store: store, // ← shared across instances
));
```

### How it works

Each hit runs a single Lua script — INCR + conditional PEXPIRE + PTTL — in one round-trip. INCR is atomic so counts never drift, and PEXPIRE only fires when a new window starts, so every instance agrees on the same resetAt.

| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
| RedisRateLimitStore.connect({host, port, prefix}) | Opens a connection to a Redis server; the prefix namespaces keys. |
| store.hit(key, window) | Records a hit; returns count + resetAt |
| store.reset(key) | Clears the counter for key |
| store.close() | Releases the Redis connection |

See: [See: core rateLimit() middleware](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=middleware-builtin)

## darto_mailer
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-mailer -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_mailer)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_mailer)

Email sending — a small Mailer API with an SMTP transport (pure-Dart) plus console and memory transports for development and tests.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_mailer: ^1.0.0
```

### Send via SMTP

```dart
import 'package:darto_mailer/darto_mailer.dart';

final mailer = Mailer(
  from: 'no-reply@example.com',
  transport: SmtpTransport(
    host: 'smtp.example.com',
    port: 587,
    username: env.smtpUser,
    password: env.smtpPass,
    security: SmtpSecurity.starttls, // none | ssl | starttls
  ),
);

await mailer.send(Message(
  to: 'user@example.com',
  subject: 'Welcome!',
  text: 'Hello, welcome aboard.',
  html: '<h1>Hello!</h1>',
));
```

### Cc / bcc and attachments

```dart
await mailer.send(Message(
  to: ['a@x.com', 'b@x.com'],
  cc: 'manager@x.com',
  replyTo: 'support@x.com',
  subject: 'Report',
  html: '<p>See attached.</p>',
  attachments: [
    Attachment.file('report.pdf'),
    Attachment.bytes('logo.png', bytes, contentType: 'image/png'),
  ],
));
```

### Dev & test transports

```dart
// Dev — prints a summary, sends nothing
final mailer = Mailer(from: '…', transport: ConsoleTransport());

// Tests — capture and assert
final box = MemoryTransport();
final mailer = Mailer(from: 'a@b.com', transport: box);
await mailer.send(Message(to: 'x@y.com', subject: 'Hi', text: '…'));
expect(box.sent.single.message.subject, 'Hi');
```

> **Tip:** No template engine here — pass rendered html/text. Combine with darto_view (Mustache/Jinja) to render the body.

| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Mailer({from, transport}) | Sends messages; injects the default from |
| Message({to, cc, bcc, replyTo, subject, text, html, attachments}) | An email |
| Attachment.file / .bytes / .string | Attachment constructors |
| SmtpTransport({host, port, username, password, security}) | SMTP delivery |
| SmtpSecurity | none / ssl / starttls |
| ConsoleTransport / MemoryTransport | Dev / test transports |

## darto_jobs
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-jobs -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_jobs)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_jobs)

Background job queue — enqueue work, process it with retries and backoff, backed by an in-memory or Redis store (at-least-once).

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_jobs: ^1.0.0
```

### Define, enqueue, work

```dart
import 'package:darto_jobs/darto_jobs.dart';

final queue = JobQueue(store: MemoryJobStore()); // or RedisJobStore.connect(...)

queue.handle('send-welcome', (job) async {
  await mailer.send(Message(to: job.data['email'], subject: 'Welcome'));
});

await queue.add('send-welcome', {'email': 'user@x.com'});
await queue.add('report', {'id': 42}, delay: Duration(minutes: 5));

final worker = queue.work(concurrency: 4);
// … on shutdown:
await worker.stop(); // drains in-flight jobs
```

### Retries, backoff & dead-letter

```dart
queue.handle('charge', (job) async {
  await payments.charge(job.data['orderId']);
}, maxAttempts: 5, backoff: (attempt) => Duration(seconds: 2 * attempt));

queue.onFailed((job, error, stack) {
  log.error('job ${job.name} gave up', error, stack);
});

final dead = await queue.store.deadLetter();
```

> **Warning:** Delivery is at-least-once — a job may run more than once if a worker crashes mid-process. Make handlers idempotent.

### Durable & distributed (Redis)

RedisJobStore persists jobs across restarts and lets multiple worker processes share one queue. reserve runs a Lua script (promote due → pop → lease) atomically; a periodic sweep re-queues jobs from crashed workers.

```dart
final queue = JobQueue(
  store: await RedisJobStore.connect(host: 'localhost', port: 6379),
);
```

| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
| JobQueue({store}) | add / handle / work / onFailed / close |
| queue.add(name, data, {delay, scheduledAt, maxAttempts}) | Enqueue a job |
| queue.handle(name, handler, {maxAttempts, backoff}) | Register a handler |
| queue.work({concurrency, pollInterval, lease}) | Start a Worker |
| Worker.stop() | Drain in-flight jobs and stop |
| MemoryJobStore / RedisJobStore | In-process / durable, shared store |
| JobStats | ready / delayed / active / dead counts |

## darto_ws
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-ws -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_ws)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_ws)

WebSocket support — same port, route-integrated. Middleware (auth, params, state) runs before the upgrade. v1.1 adds rooms / broadcast (WsHub) and a Redis adapter for multi-instance fanout.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_ws: ^1.1.0
```

See: [Full guide: WebSocket](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=websocket)

## darto_view
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-view -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_view)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_view)

Pluggable template engine — ships with Mustache. Register once, then call c.render() in any handler.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_view: ^1.0.1
  mustache_template: ^2.0.0
```

See: [Full guide: View Engine](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=view-engine)

## darto_static
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-static -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_static)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_static)

Static file serving middleware — ETag, 304, Range requests, optional gzip and Cache-Control, with built-in path-traversal protection.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_static: ^1.0.1
```

### Usage

```dart
import 'package:darto_static/darto_static.dart';

app.mount('/public/*', serveStatic('public'));
app.mount('/assets/*', serveStatic('dist', urlPrefix: '/assets', maxAge: Duration(days: 7)));
```

### serveStatic options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| dir (positional) | Directory to serve files from (relative to cwd) |
| urlPrefix | URL prefix to strip — defaults to '/dir'. Set when the mount path differs from the folder name. |
| maxAge | Sets Cache-Control: public, max-age=N. Omit to disable client caching. |

> **Tip:** Built-in path-traversal protection, automatic MIME detection, and ETag / 304 / Range support. When a file isn't found it falls through to next(), so your other routes still match.

## darto_env
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-env -->

- [pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/darto_env)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/evandersondev/darto/tree/main/darto_env)

Environment variable loader — reads .env files and Platform.environment with typed accessors.

### Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  darto_env: ^1.0.1
```

### Usage

```dart
import 'package:darto_env/darto_env.dart';

DartoEnv.load(); // call once at startup

final port  = DartoEnv.getInt('PORT', 3000);
final debug = DartoEnv.getBool('DEBUG', false);
final key   = DartoEnv.get('API_KEY'); // throws if missing
```

### API

| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| DartoEnv.load([path = '.env']) | Load a .env file — call once at startup |
| DartoEnv.get('KEY', [default]) | String — throws if missing and no default given |
| DartoEnv.maybeGet('KEY') | String? — null when the var is not set |
| DartoEnv.getInt('PORT', 3000) | int with a fallback |
| DartoEnv.getDouble('RATE', 1.5) | double with a fallback |
| DartoEnv.getBool('DEBUG', false) | bool with a fallback |
| DartoEnv.getOrThrow('KEY') | String — always throws if missing |
| DartoEnv.all() | Map<String, String> — every loaded variable |


---

# Advanced

## Render / Layouts
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=render-layouts -->

Two-step rendering modelled after Hono's setRenderer / c.render.

```dart
app.use((Context c, Next next) async {
  c.setRender((content, props) => c.html('''
    <!DOCTYPE html>
    <html><head><title>${props['title'] ?? 'Darto'}</title></head>
    <body>$content</body></html>
  '''));
  await next();
});

app.get('/', [], (c) => c.render('<h1>Welcome</h1>', {'title': 'Home'}));
```

> **Tip:** Register a different layout via app.mount('/admin/*', …) to override for a path scope.

## View Engine
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=view-engine -->

See: [Package: darto_view](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-view)

For file-based templates (Mustache, Jinja…) use the darto_view package. Register an engine once via middleware, then call c.render() in any handler.

```dart
import 'package:darto/darto.dart';
import 'package:darto_view/darto_view.dart';

final app = Darto();
app.use(viewEngine(MustacheEngine(viewsPath: 'views')));

app.get('/', [], (c) => c.render('index', {
  'title': 'Home',
  'items': ['Routing', 'Middleware', 'Validation'],
}));
```

### Template file

Templates live in the viewsPath directory (here views/) with a .mustache extension. c.render('index', data) renders views/index.mustache with data.

```html
// views/index.mustache
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <head><title>{{title}}</title></head>
  <body>
    <h1>{{title}}</h1>
    <ul>
      {{#items}}<li>{{.}}</li>{{/items}}
    </ul>
  </body>
</html>
```

## File Upload
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=file-upload -->

### In-memory

```dart
app.post('/upload', [], (c) async {
  final form = await c.req.formData();
  final file = (form as Map)['avatar'] as UploadedFile;
  print(file.filename); // logo.png
  print(file.size);     // bytes
  return c.created({'name': file.filename});
});
```

### Streamed to disk

```dart
app.post('/upload', [], (c) async {
  final form = await c.req.formData();
  final file = (form as Map)['video'] as UploadedFile;
  await file.saveTo('uploads/${file.filename}');
  return c.created({'ok': true});
});
```

> **Tip:** Prefer saveTo for files larger than a few MB — it streams instead of buffering the entire body.

## File Download
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=file-download -->

```dart
// Inline (browser tries to render it)
await c.file('/path/to/report.pdf');

// Force download with Content-Disposition
await c.download('/path/to/report.csv', filename: 'export.csv');
```

## WebSocket
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=websocket -->

See: [Package: darto_ws](https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=plugin-ws)

Use the darto_ws package to upgrade any route to a WebSocket — same port, same middleware pipeline as HTTP routes. Add it to your pubspec: darto_ws: ^1.1.0.

```dart
import 'package:darto/darto.dart';
import 'package:darto_ws/darto_ws.dart';

app.get('/chat', [], upgradeWebSocket((c) => WSHandler(
  onOpen:    (ws) => ws.send('hello'),
  onMessage: (event, ws) => ws.send('echo: ${event.text}'),
  onClose:   (ws) => print('${ws.id} disconnected'),
)));
```

### Path params and state

```dart
// Middleware runs before the upgrade — path params and state are available inside WSHandler
app.get('/rooms/:id', [bearerAuth(token: env.token)], upgradeWebSocket((c) {
  final room = c.req.param('id');
  return WSHandler(
    onOpen: (ws) => ws.send('joined room $room'),
    onMessage: (event, ws) => ws.sendJson({'echo': event.json}),
  );
}));
```

> **Success:** Middleware (auth, logging) runs before the upgrade — protect WS endpoints just like HTTP routes.

### Rooms and broadcast — WsHub

A WsHub is the connection registry — group sockets in rooms and fan messages out. Install one per app via middleware so every upgradeWebSocket factory picks it up automatically.

```dart
final hub = WsHub();
final app = Darto()..use(hub.middleware());

app.get('/chat/:room', [], upgradeWebSocket((c) {
  final room = c.req.param('room')!;
  return WSHandler(
    onOpen: (ws) {
      ws.join(room);
      ws.to(room).except(ws).send('${ws.id} joined');
    },
    onMessage: (ev, ws) =>
      ws.to(room).sendJson({'from': ws.id, 'text': ev.text}),
    onClose: (ws) { /* ws.leave(room) is automatic */ },
  );
}));

// Server-initiated broadcast — works from any HTTP route or cron
app.post('/announce', [], (c) async {
  hub.to('lobby').send('shutdown soon');
  return c.noContent();
});
```

| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
| hub.middleware() | Exposes the hub to factories via wsHub(c) |
| hub.to(room) / hub.broadcast() | Fluent fanout — chain .except(ws), then send / sendJson / sendBytes |
| hub.connections / roomSize / rooms | Membership stats |
| ws.id / ws.rooms | Per-connection UUID + rooms it is in |
| ws.join(room) / ws.leave(room) | Mutate membership (auto-leave on close) |
| ws.to(room) / ws.broadcast() | Shortcuts to hub.to / hub.broadcast |

### Multi-instance fanout — RedisWsAdapter

When two replicas behind a load balancer hold sockets in the same room, attach the Redis adapter so broadcasts cross instances. Origin-id tagging suppresses self-echo.

```dart
final hub = WsHub();
await hub.attachAdapter(await RedisWsAdapter.connect(
  host: 'localhost',
  port: 6379,
));

final app = Darto()..use(hub.middleware());
```

### WSHandler & DartoWebSocket

| WSHandler callback | Description |
| --- | --- |
| onOpen: (ws) | Connection established |
| onMessage: (event, ws) | A frame arrived — read event.text or event.json |
| onClose: (ws) | Connection closed — read ws.id / ws.rooms here |
| onError: (err, ws) | An error occurred |

> **Warning:** Breaking in 1.1.0: onClose and onError now receive the closing socket — old `onClose: () => …` becomes `onClose: (_) => …`.

| ws / event member | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ws.send(text) | Send a text frame to this client |
| ws.sendJson(map) | Encode a Map as JSON and send it |
| ws.sendBytes(bytes) | Send a binary frame |
| ws.close([code, reason]) | Close the connection |
| ws.closeCode | Peer close code (null while open) |
| ws.id | Unique per-connection UUID v4 |
| ws.rooms / join / leave / to / broadcast | Hub helpers (require hub.middleware()) |
| event.text | Frame data as a String |
| event.json | Frame data parsed as a JSON Map |

## Error Handling
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=error-handling -->

```dart
app.onError((err, c) {
  print('error: $err');
  return c.internalError({'error': 'something went wrong'});
});

app.notFound((c) => c.notFound({'error': 'route not found'}));
```

## Full Example
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=full-example -->

```dart
import 'package:darto/darto.dart';
import 'package:darto/logger.dart';
import 'package:darto/cors.dart';
import 'package:darto/jwt.dart';
import 'package:darto_validator/darto_validator.dart';

final createUser = z.map({
  'email': z.string().email(),
  'name':  z.string().min(2),
});

void main() {
  final app = Darto();

  app.use(logger());
  app.mount('/api/*', cors());
  app.mount('/api/*', jwt(secret: 'mySecret'));

  final api = app.group('/api');

  api.get('/me', [], (c) {
    final payload = c.get<Map<String, dynamic>>('jwtPayload');
    return c.ok({'userId': payload?['sub']});
  });

  api.post('/users', [zValidator('json', createUser)], (c) {
    final data = c.req.valid<Map<String, dynamic>>('json');
    return c.created({'id': '42', ...data});
  });

  app.onError((err, c) => c.internalError({'error': err.toString()}));

  app.listen(3000, () => print('http://localhost:3000'));
}
```


---

# Migration Guide

## v0.x → v2 Overview
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=migration-overview -->

> **Warning:** Darto v2 is a full rewrite. The API is incompatible with v0.x. Every route, middleware and response call needs to be updated. This guide covers all breaking changes side by side.

The central concept changed from Express-style (Request, Response, NextFunction) to a Hono-style single Context object. Everything request/response related is now on c.

| Area | v0.x | v2 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Handler | (Request req, Response res) | (Context c) |
| Middleware | (Request req, Response res, NextFunction next) | (Context c, Next next) async |
| Route verb | app.get(path, handler) | app.get(path, [], handler) |
| Path-scoped mw | app.use('/path', mw) | app.mount('/path', mw) |
| Path params | req.param['id'] / req.params['id'] | c.req.param('id') |
| Query params | req.query['key'] | c.req.query('key') |
| Body | await req.body | await c.req.json() |
| Send JSON | res.json({...}) | c.json({...}) / c.ok({...}) |
| Status + send | res.status(201).json({...}) | c.status(201).json({...}) / c.created({...}) |
| Error handler | (Err, Request, Response, Next) | app.onError((err, c) => ...) |

## Handler & Middleware
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=migration-handlers -->

### Handler signature

```dart
// v0.x
app.get('/users/:id', (Request req, Response res) {
  final id = req.param['id'];
  res.json({'id': id});
});

// v2
app.get('/users/:id', [], (Context c) {
  final id = c.req.param('id');
  return c.ok({'id': id});
});
```

### Middleware signature

```dart
// v0.x
app.use((Request req, Response res, NextFunction next) {
  print('request: ${req.method} ${req.originalUrl}');
  next();
});

// v2
app.use((Context c, Next next) async {
  print('request: ${c.req.method} ${c.req.path}');
  await next();
});
```

### Route-level middleware

```dart
// v0.x — middleware passed as positional argument before handler
app.get('/admin', authMiddleware, (req, res) { ... });

// v2 — always a List in the second argument
app.get('/admin', [authMiddleware], (c) { ... });
```

## Request API
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=migration-request -->

```dart
// v0.x
final id      = req.param['id'];          // path param
final name    = req.query['name'];         // query param
final body    = await req.body;            // any body
final auth    = req.headers.value('authorization');
final method  = req.method;
final path    = req.originalUrl;

// v2
final id      = c.req.param('id');         // path param
final name    = c.req.query('name');       // query param
final body    = await c.req.json();        // JSON body
final typed   = await c.req.json<User>(User.fromJson);
final auth    = c.req.header('authorization');
final method  = c.req.method;
final path    = c.req.path;
```

> **Tip:** c.req.queryInt() and c.req.queryBool() parse typed query params without manual conversion.

## Response API
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=migration-response -->

```dart
// v0.x
res.send('text');               // plain text
res.json({'key': 'value'});     // JSON
res.status(201).json({...});    // status + JSON
res.status(404).send('Not found');
res.redirect('https://example.com');
res.sendFile('path/to/file');
res.download('path/to/file', {'filename': 'custom.txt'});

// v2
return c.text('text');          // plain text
return c.json({'key': 'value'});
return c.ok({...});             // 200 JSON
return c.created({...});        // 201 JSON
return c.badRequest({...});     // 400 JSON
return c.notFound({...});       // 404 JSON
return c.status(201).json({...});
return c.redirect('https://example.com');
await c.file('path/to/file');   // serve inline
await c.download('path', filename: 'custom.txt');
```

| v0.x | v2 equivalent |
| --- | --- |
| res.send('text') | c.text('text') |
| res.json({...}) | c.json({...}) |
| res.status(200).json({...}) | c.ok({...}) |
| res.status(201).json({...}) | c.created({...}) |
| res.status(400).json({...}) | c.badRequest({...}) |
| res.status(401).json({...}) | c.unauthorized({...}) |
| res.status(403).json({...}) | c.forbidden({...}) |
| res.status(404).send(...) | c.notFound({...}) |
| res.status(500).send(...) | c.internalError({...}) |
| res.redirect(url) | c.redirect(url) |
| res.sendFile(path) | await c.file(path) |
| res.download(path, opts) | await c.download(path, filename: ...) |

## Routing & Middleware Registration
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=migration-routing -->

### Path-scoped middleware

```dart
// v0.x
app.use('/api/:id', middlewareFn); // path-scoped

// v2
app.mount('/api/*', middlewareFn); // wildcard required for prefix matching
```

### Router / grouping

```dart
// v0.x
Router userRouter() {
  final r = Router();
  r.get('/', (req, res) { res.json(users); });
  r.post('/', (req, res) async { ... });
  return r;
}
app.use('/users', userRouter());

// v2 — three equivalent styles
// 1. Fluent chain
app.route('/users').get([], listUsers).post([], createUser);

// 2. Builder callback
app.route('/users', (r) {
  r.get('/', [], listUsers);
  r.post('/', [], createUser);
});

// 3. group() prefix
final users = app.group('/users');
users.get('/', [], listUsers);
users.post('/', [], createUser);
```

### Error handler

```dart
// v0.x
app.use((Err err, Request req, Response res, NextFunction next) {
  res.status(500).json({'error': err.toString()});
});

// v2
app.onError((err, c) {
  return c.internalError({'error': err.toString()});
});
```

## Validation
<!-- https://darto-docs.vercel.app/docs?section=migration-validation -->

```dart
// v0.x — manual try/catch with Zard
app.post('/users', (Request req, Response res) async {
  final schema = z.map({
    'name': z.string().min(3),
    'age':  z.int().min(1),
  });
  try {
    final data = await schema.parseAsync(req.body);
    res.json(data);
  } catch (e) {
    res.status(406).send(schema.getErrors());
  }
});

// v2 — zValidator middleware (darto_validator package)
import 'package:darto_validator/darto_validator.dart';

final userSchema = z.map({
  'name': z.string().min(3),
  'age':  z.int().min(1),
});

app.post('/users', [zValidator('json', userSchema)], (c) {
  final data = c.req.valid<Map<String, dynamic>>('json');
  return c.created(data);
});
```

> **Tip:** Add darto_validator: ^1.1.0 to pubspec.yaml. zard is re-exported — no separate zard dependency needed.
